steps.title: Recognize the sentence structure and the subject being referred to
steps.content:
The first sentence establishes “the constitution of Australia” as the subject.

In the second sentence, after the introductory phrase ending with a comma, the blank needs to refer back to this same singular subject.

The structure “_____ 102nd in a global ranking” requires a pronoun that restates the subject plus a verb form.

steps.title: Apply the correct pronoun and verb form
steps.content:
”It's” (it is) is correct: “it” is the singular pronoun referring to “the constitution” and “is” is the copular verb needed to connect the subject to the predicate adjective “102nd.” The other choices fail: “their” and “they're” are plural forms; “its” is possessive without a verb form.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
